content.text: The EP-133 K.O. II is a 64 MB sampler and composer from Teenage Engineering. It features 999 sample slots, 16 mono or 12 stereo voice polyphony, and a unique calculator-inspired interface. OS 2.0 introduces song mode, resampling, hands-free sampling, and enhanced punch-in effects.

content.name: 999 Sample Slots
content.description: Store up to 999 samples across all projects. Samples are recorded at 46.875 kHz / 16-bit with a 32-bit float signal chain.

content.name: 4 Groups with 12 Sounds Each
content.description: Each project has 4 groups (A-D), and each group has 12 assignable pads (0-9, +, -). Groups play simultaneously for layered arrangements.

content.name: Song Mode
content.description: Chain up to 99 song positions with different pattern arrangements to create complete songs up to 9,801 bars long.

content.name: Punch-In FX 2.0
content.description: 12 pressure-sensitive punch-in effects for live performance. Hold number keys to instantly apply beat repeat, filter sweeps, reverb freeze, and more.

content.text: The LCD display shows key information including the current bar position (BAR), tempo in BPM, stereo/mono indicator, current sound number, FX status, quantize setting (Q), and swing amount. The display content changes based on the current mode and operation.

content.caption: LCD display showing bar position, tempo, and status indicators

content.text: The EP-133 has three main knobs with distinct colors indicating their function: the white VOLUME knob controls output level, the orange GAIN knob (also labeled X) controls input level for sampling, and the black Y knob is a context-sensitive parameter control.

content.caption: The three main knobs: Volume (white), Gain/X (orange), and Y (black)

content.text: Use MAIN/COMMIT to return to the main view at any time. The +/- buttons navigate between values, patterns, sounds, and menu items. Press ENTER to confirm selections after entering numbers.

items: MAIN/COMMIT - Return to main view (tap) or save changes (hold)
+ / - - Increment or decrement values, navigate menus
ENTER - Confirm selection or numeric entry
Number keys (0-9) - Direct entry for sounds, patterns, and values

content.text: Hold the SHIFT button (bottom-left corner) to access secondary functions on most buttons. The secondary function is typically printed below the primary function label in smaller text.

content.title: Essential Shift Combinations
content.text: SHIFT + ERASE opens System menu. SHIFT + Group button mutes/unmutes that group. SHIFT + SAMPLE enables resampling mode.

The ERASE button is used to delete notes, sounds, and patterns. Hold ERASE and press a pad to delete that step from the sequence. Hold ERASE and press a group button to clear that group's entire pattern.

content.title: Caution
content.text: Erased data cannot be recovered unless you have not committed the changes. Press MAIN without holding (don't commit) to discard changes.
The EP-133 received a major OS 2.0 update (current version 2.0.5) that added several significant features:

items: Resampling - Record the EP-133's output as a new sample
Song Mode - Chain patterns into complete song arrangements
Hands-Free Sampling - Automatic recording triggered by audio threshold
Sidechain - Ducking effect between sounds for pumping bass
Enhanced Polyphony - 16 mono or 12 stereo voices (up from 12/6)
MIDI Thru - Pass MIDI through to other devices
Punch-In FX 2.0 - Improved pressure-sensitive performance effects
